answer:
\boxed{J}

Garrison’s argument that free Black Americans were entitled to full citizenship rights under the Constitution was directly contradicted by *Dred Scott v. Sanford (1857)*, which ruled that enslaved and free Black people were not—and could never be—U.S. citizens. This decision reinforced racial exclusion, undermining Garrison’s belief in constitutional protections for free people of color. No other listed case addresses citizenship for free Black individuals in this way.
